caption: First day of outpatient treatment. The fluorescent lights feel harsh and the waiting room smells like bleach. I don't want to be here but I have no choice.
Stayed in bed most of the day. My apartment feels like a cage. Ate cereal at 3pm because I forgot about lunch.
Therapist said I need to get outside. Made it to the lagoon but sat on a bench for five minutes and left. Everything feels heavy.
Volunteered at the soup kitchen today because my counselor suggested it. Seeing people worse off didn't make me feel better. It made me feel guilty for feeling bad.
Follow-up appointment. New medication feels like it's making me dizzy. The doctor keeps asking me to rate my mood on a scale. I said four.
Went to Sunday service for the first time in years. I couldn't focus on anything the pastor said but the music made me cry. Not sure if that's good or bad.
Forced myself to have coffee somewhere other than my kitchen. The barista asked how I was doing and I almost told her the truth. Said fine instead.
Went back to the park. Stayed longer this time. Watched some kids playing on the swings and remembered what it felt like to not carry anything.
Group session at the community center. Met someone who's been sober for two years. She looked normal. Like someone I'd see at the grocery store.
Had dinner out alone. Read a book at the table. The waiter didn't judge me for being solo. Pasta tasted better than anything I've had in months.
Spent two hours at the library. Found a quiet corner and just read. My mind didn't race for the first time in weeks.
Monthly check-in. Doctor said my bloodwork looks better. I mentioned I've been sleeping more than four hours. She seemed genuinely happy for me.
Walked a trail at Kincaid. The snow was fresh and nobody else was around. The quiet didn't feel lonely this time. It felt chosen.
Back at church. Actually listened to the sermon this time. Something about grace being unearned. That hit differently.
Morning coffee at Middle Way. Brought my journal. Wrote three pages without stopping. My handwriting is getting steadier.
Shared my story at group for the first time. My voice shook but I got through it. Three people came up to me afterward. We're having coffee next week.
Sat in the sun at Delaney. A stranger's dog came up to me and I laughed. Actually laughed. It surprised me.
Dinner with the people from group. We talked about normal things. Movies. Weather. It felt like having friends again.
Group therapy at the hospital. Shared my progress and someone asked me for advice. That felt strange. Good strange.
Hiked Flattop. Made it halfway before my lungs gave out but I didn't care. The view was worth every step.
Back at the soup kitchen. This time I actually talked to people. Heard stories that made mine feel less unique. We're all just trying.
Started a new book about neuroscience. My ability to focus has improved dramatically. I can sit still for an hour without checking my phone.
Tried a different church this week. Smaller congregation. People actually shook my hand and asked my name. Belonging is a strange feeling to rediscover.
Morning routine now. Coffee, journal, 30 minutes of just being. The barista knows my order. That small thing matters more than I expected.
Russian Jack Springs. The creek was thawing. Something about watching frozen things come back to life felt personal.
Led the opening share at group tonight. Four months ago I couldn't make eye contact. Tonight I stood in front of twenty people and spoke from the heart.
Celebrated three months of progress with dinner at Orso. I'm not fixed. But I'm moving. And the food tastes real again.
Intake day. My hands were shaking so bad the nurse had to help me fill out the paperwork. She didn't comment on it. Small mercy.
Woke up at my sister's place. She made pancakes but I couldn't eat more than two bites. The effort of chewing felt exhausting.
Walked past my old bar tonight. My feet took me there without thinking. Stood outside for ten minutes. Didn't go in. That counts as a win.
Sister dragged me to the lagoon. I complained the entire walk there. But the geese on the water looked peaceful and I stopped complaining.
Therapy session. We talked about triggers. Apparently smells are my biggest one. The detergent my ex used. Cooking smoke. Bar soap.
Went grocery shopping alone for the first time. Had a panic attack in the cereal aisle. Left the cart and sat in my car for twenty minutes. Then went back in and finished.
My sponsor suggested church. I'm not religious but I went anyway. The stained glass was beautiful. I focused on that instead of the words.
Cooked a full meal tonight. Nothing fancy. Rice and vegetables. But I cooked it and ate it at the table instead of over the sink. Progress.
Point Woronzof. Watched planes take off. Each one going somewhere new. I'm not going anywhere yet but at least I'm watching.
First time at the gym in over a year. Did fifteen minutes on the treadmill and thought I'd die. But I showed up. My body remembers how to move.
Therapist says I'm making progress even though it doesn't feel like it. She pointed out I made eye contact three times today. I hadn't noticed.
Coffee shop. Brought my sketch pad. Drew the view from the window. First time I've drawn anything in months. My lines are shaky but they're mine.
Walked past the bar district again. This time on purpose. Felt the pull but kept walking. My feet are learning new routes.
Chester Creek trail. Ran into another person from my therapy group. We walked together for a mile without talking. Comfortable silence with another person hasn't happened in so long.
AA meeting at the community center. Thirty days sober today. They gave me a chip. It's just plastic but I've been holding it in my pocket all evening.
Thirty minutes on the treadmill now. Doubled my time in three weeks. My body is waking up. So is something else. Ambition maybe.
Grocery shopping without anxiety today. Bought ingredients for a recipe I found online. Even chatted with the checkout person.
Different church this week. The pastor talked about forgiveness. I'm not there yet with myself but I didn't shut down when I heard the word.

Kincaid Park at sunset. The sky was impossible shades of pink. I took this photo and realized it's the first photo I've taken because something was beautiful, not because I was documenting evidence of being alive.
Dinner with my sponsor and two others from the program. Ordered dessert. Stayed for two hours. Laughed until my stomach hurt. I'd forgotten what that felt like.
Back at my own apartment. Not my sister's. Mine. Hung a picture on the wall. Opened the curtains. The light came in and I let it.
Psychiatric evaluation. They asked me a hundred questions. On a scale of one to ten how often do you feel hopeless. Every number felt wrong.
The medication makes mornings blurry. I stared at the ceiling for an hour before I could convince my legs to move.
Delaney Park. The cold air woke me up more than the coffee did. Watched people walking their dogs. Everyone seems to have something to care for.
Found a free art therapy class. They gave me paints and said make whatever you feel. I painted a grey rectangle. The instructor said that was valid.
Medication adjustment. The new one tastes chalky. Doctor says it takes two weeks to know if it works. Two weeks feels like a lifetime right now.
Sat in a coffee shop and watched people work on laptops. They all looked so sure of what they're doing. I ordered chamomile because caffeine makes my anxiety worse.
Earthquake Park. The interpretive signs talk about how the earth literally broke apart in 1964 and then rebuilt itself. I read every single one.
Week two of art therapy. I painted something with color today. Blue and white. The instructor said it looked like the ocean. I said it was just blue.
Mental health support group. Everyone introduces themselves by first name only. No last names. No job titles. Just a name and whatever you want to share.
Cleaned the kitchen today. Not because it was dirty. Because I wanted to. That distinction matters even if nobody else would understand it.
Valentine's Day alone but not sad about it. Russian Jack park was empty. I built a tiny snowman and left it on a bench. Gift for whoever finds it.
The new medication is working. I told the doctor that colors seem brighter. She said that's a common description. It felt personal though.
Painted flowers today. Not realistic ones. Wild messy flowers with too many petals. The instructor put it on the wall. First time anything I made has been on a wall.
Coffee shop again. This time I brought a sketchbook. Drew the coffee cup. Then drew the plant on the windowsill. Then drew the person sitting across from me. They noticed and smiled.
Support group. Someone new joined and looked terrified. I remembered feeling exactly like that three weeks ago. I sat next to them and said hi.
Flattop mountain. Only made it a quarter of the way up but stood there and looked at the whole city spread out below. Everything looks smaller from above. Problems included.

Anchorage museum. Spent an hour in the Alaska Native art section. The craftsmanship in those pieces. Generations of skill and patience and care. I want to make something with that kind of intentionality.
Started a larger piece today. Canvas instead of paper. The instructor is letting me take it home to keep working on it. I have a project. A reason to look forward to tomorrow.
Dinner with two people from the support group. We went to a real restaurant. Not fast food. We ordered appetizers. I felt like a person who does normal things.
Sunset at Point Woronzof. Brought my sketchbook and tried to capture the colors. Failed completely. The sky doesn't fit on paper. But trying felt like enough.
Doctor's visit. She asked what's changed. I said I have a painting on a wall and friends who eat appetizers. She wrote something down and smiled.

Led the group activity today. Brought art supplies and we all made collages. The new person from three weeks ago made something beautiful. They said I inspired them. I've never inspired anyone before.
